From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Actor and commercial fisherman (1922–2005)

For the Boston merchant and benefactor of the Boston Athenæum, see [John Bromfield Jr.](/wiki/John_Bromfield_Jr. "John Bromfield Jr.") For Sir John Bromfield, Baronet, see [Bromfield baronets](/wiki/Bromfield_baronets "Bromfield baronets").

| John Bromfield | |
| --- | --- |
| Bromfield as Frank Morgan. | |
| Born | Farron Bromfield  (1922-06-11)June 11, 1922  [South Bend, Indiana](/wiki/South_Bend,_Indiana "South Bend, Indiana"), U.S. |
| Died | September 19, 2005(2005-09-19) (aged 83)  [Palm Desert, California](/wiki/Palm_Desert,_California "Palm Desert, California"), U.S. |
| Resting place | [Forest Lawn Memorial Park, Glendale, California](/wiki/Forest_Lawn_Memorial_Park_(Glendale) "Forest Lawn Memorial Park (Glendale)") |
| Occupations | Actor, commercial fisherman |
| Years active | 1948–1960 |
| Spouses | Grace Landis  ​ (divorced)​      [Corinne Calvet](/wiki/Corinne_Calvet "Corinne Calvet")  ​  ​ (m. 1948; div. 1954)​      [Larri Thomas](/wiki/Larri_Thomas "Larri Thomas")  ​  ​ (m. 1955; div. 1959)​      [Mary Ellen Bromfield](/wiki/Mary_Ellen_Bromfield "Mary Ellen Bromfield")  ​  ​ (m. 1962⁠–⁠2005)​ |

**John Bromfield** (born **Farron Bromfield**;[[1]](#cite_note-1) June 11, 1922 – September 19, 2005) was an American actor and commercial fisherman.

## Early years

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Bromfield&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early years")]

Farron Bromfield was born in [South Bend, Indiana](/wiki/South_Bend,_Indiana "South Bend, Indiana"). He played [football](/wiki/American_football "American football") and was a [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") champion[[2]](#cite_note-2) at [Saint Mary's College of California](/wiki/Saint_Mary%27s_College_of_California "Saint Mary's College of California"), where he also lettered in football, baseball, track and swimming.[[3]](#cite_note-tdc-3) In the 1940s, he gained his first acting experience at the [La Jolla Playhouse](/wiki/La_Jolla_Playhouse "La Jolla Playhouse").[[4]](#cite_note-opa-4)

Bromfield served in the [United States Navy](/wiki/United_States_Navy "United States Navy") in World War II.

## Film

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Bromfield&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Film")]

Bromfield's screen debut came in *[Harpoon](/wiki/Harpoon_(1948_film) "Harpoon (1948 film)")* (1948).[[4]](#cite_note-opa-4) The same year, he was cast as a detective in *[Sorry, Wrong Number](/wiki/Sorry,_Wrong_Number "Sorry, Wrong Number")*, starring [Burt Lancaster](/wiki/Burt_Lancaster "Burt Lancaster") and [Barbara Stanwyck](/wiki/Barbara_Stanwyck "Barbara Stanwyck") for [Paramount Pictures](/wiki/Paramount_Pictures "Paramount Pictures"). In 1953, Bromfield appeared with [Esther Williams](/wiki/Esther_Williams "Esther Williams"), [Van Johnson](/wiki/Van_Johnson "Van Johnson") and [Tony Martin](/wiki/Tony_Martin_(American_singer) "Tony Martin (American singer)") in *[Easy to Love](/wiki/Easy_to_Love_(1953_film) "Easy to Love (1953 film)")*.

He also starred in horror films, including the 1955 3D production *[Revenge of the Creature](/wiki/Revenge_of_the_Creature "Revenge of the Creature")*, one of the *[Creature from the Black Lagoon](/wiki/Creature_from_the_Black_Lagoon "Creature from the Black Lagoon")* sequels.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Television

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Bromfield&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Television")]

In the middle 1950s, Bromfield appeared in westerns, such as [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC")'s *[Frontier](/wiki/Frontier_(1955_TV_series) "Frontier (1955 TV series)")* [anthology series](/wiki/Anthology_series "Anthology series") in the role of a [sheriff](/wiki/Sheriff "Sheriff") in the episode "The Hanging at Thunder Butte Creek".[[6]](#cite_note-etvs-6)[[7]](#cite_note-obit-7)

## Later years

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Bromfield&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Later years")]

In 1960, Bromfield retired from acting to produce sports shows and work as a commercial fisherman off [Newport Beach, California](/wiki/Newport_Beach,_California "Newport Beach, California").

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Bromfield&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Bromfield married Grace Landis while in college. They later divorced. He married actress [Corinne Calvet](/wiki/Corinne_Calvet "Corinne Calvet") in Boulder, Colorado, in 1948. They were divorced March 16, 1954.[[8]](#cite_note-8) He also was divorced from actress/dancer [Larri Thomas](/wiki/Larri_Thomas "Larri Thomas"). He and his fourth wife, actress/dancer and author [Mary Ellen Bromfield](/wiki/Mary_Ellen_Bromfield "Mary Ellen Bromfield"), were married 43 years.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Bromfield&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Death")]

Bromfield died September 18, 2005, at age 83 from [renal failure](/wiki/Renal_failure "Renal failure") in [Palm Desert, California](/wiki/Palm_Desert,_California "Palm Desert, California").[[4]](#cite_note-opa-4)
